title: A question on Kaworu involving Third Impact
display_title: A question on Kaworu involving Third Impact
wikitext:
[]: A question on Kaworu involving Third Impact.             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Sun Apr 08, 2012 3:26 pm I'm hoping to be able to get some answers on this since I'm semi-new to Evangelion as I was introduced to it last year but have recently gotten back into it and an trying to understand it more. Basically, since at first Kaworu intended to cause Third Impact by joining/awakening Adam again in Terminal Dogma, which turned out to be Lilith... How would him joining with Lilith cause Third impact? Rei joins with Lilith in the film, but Rei has Liliths soul and the outcome would be a Lilith based third impact, as it turned out to be. What would be the outcome of Kaworu contacting Lilith and why? Also, why would Seele send Kaworu send Nerv to start Third Impact if it wouldn't be the version they wanted it to be? Apart from weakening Shinji's resolve for later on... But they can't of been certain that Nerv could have stopped Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 08, 2012 3:48 pm There's a lot of speculation in these threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2095/The-Great-Episode-24-Plot-Hole-Fanwank-Thread/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/8190/Why-did-Kaworu-think-hed-find-Adam-at-Terminal-Dogma/ But it's mainly focused on why Kaworu didn't know it was Lilith in Terminal Dogma, however a bunch of discussion about his motivations and Seele's motivations. More specific thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/341676/What-was-SEELEs-purpose-for-Kaworu/ But nobody really knows why, the details are speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Apr 08, 2012 6:05 pm My take: Nothing. Kaworu can't do anything with Lilith, and SEELE knew this. They sent him to fuck with Shinji, since they wanted him dead or useless once they put their plan into motion. Kaworu was either dumb as a box of rocks (if he was truly surprised by what he found, since SEELE told him flat out that Adam soul was in him and Adam's body was in Gendo) or knew exactly what was going on and was just playing a part for Shinji's and Rei's benefit. I suspect the latter is more likely, even though the presentation of the scene makes it hard to swallow. But that aside, what Ornette said. Nobody knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Stamp the Vashpede » Sat May 12, 2012 5:40 pm As far as I know, Kaworu was sent to NERV to "replace" Unit 02's pilot. I think this was their way of getting him to mess with Shinji's mind. Unit 01 had recently taken into itself an S2 engine. This made it in someway worthy of starting Third Impact. It makes sense to me that by removing Shinji, SEELE could get Kaworu to pilot Unit 01 with ease (as he can set his sync ratio to whatever he wants) and proceed with Third Impact faster.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Sun May 13, 2012 1:08 am Bagheera wrote: Kaworu was either dumb as a box of rocks (if he was truly surprised by what he found, [url]since SEELE told him flat out that Adam soul was in him and Adam's body was in Gendo[/url]) or knew exactly what was going on and was just playing a part for Shinji's and Rei's benefit. I suspect the latter is more likely, even though the presentation of the scene makes it hard to swallow. But that aside, what Ornette said. Nobody knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Sun May 13, 2012 4:05 pm Bagheera wrote: My take: Nothing. Kaworu can't do anything with Lilith, and SEELE knew this. They sent him to fuck with Shinji, since they wanted him dead or useless once they put their plan into motion. Kaworu was either dumb as a box of rocks (if he was truly surprised by what he found, since SEELE told him flat out that Adam soul was in him and Adam's body was in Gendo) or knew exactly what was going on and was just playing a part for Shinji's and Rei's benefit. I suspect the latter is more likely, even though the presentation of the scene makes it hard to swallow. But that aside, what Ornette said. Nobody knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 13, 2012 4:18 pm Seoul Gamer wrote: So, what would have happened had Shinji refused to kill Kaworu? He'd have still been around during End of Evangelion, and he couldn't have done anything?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 13, 2012 4:27 pm Seoul Gamer wrote: So, what would have happened had Shinji refused to kill Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun May 13, 2012 4:58 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Well, Rei was looking on significantly after deploying a super AT field of her own -- she might have intervened in some fashion if Shinji had stalled.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon May 14, 2012 3:26 pm And then NERV self-destructs.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 3:32 pm AuraTwilight wrote: And then NERV self-destructs.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Mon May 14, 2012 3:46 pm Misato says if they disappear or if they can no longer tell what is going on to blow the place, right? Don't they lose contact all of a few seconds later, but no one gives the order to self-destruct? Couldn't that be because Gendo knew nothing would happen if Koworu made it to Lillith so there was no real threat.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon May 14, 2012 7:34 pm Bagheera wrote: Probably, but again, why? Kaworu can't do anything with Lilith. He'd just sit there twiddling his thumbs feeling really awkward and stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 14, 2012 7:46 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Because no one up in the GeoFront necessarily knows that Kaworu is impotent save Gendo and Fuyutsuki, and SEELE pretty much wants everyone there dead anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue May 15, 2012 12:52 pm Gendo: Lol I've got Adam I can do manga-style AT Field bullshit and survive. Woo~!

[]:             Quote Post by EVAJedi » Tue May 15, 2012 1:42 pm sum_exsanguis wrote: Misato says if they disappear or if they can no longer tell what is going on to blow the place, right? Don't they lose contact all of a few seconds later, but no one gives the order to self-destruct? Couldn't that be because Gendo knew nothing would happen if Koworu made it to Lillith so there was no real threat.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 16, 2012 2:14 am Bagheera wrote: My take: Nothing. Kaworu can't do anything with Lilith, and SEELE knew this. They sent him to fuck with Shinji, since they wanted him dead or useless once they put their plan into motion. Kaworu was either dumb as a box of rocks (if he was truly surprised by what he found, since SEELE told him flat out that Adam soul was in him and Adam's body was in Gendo) or knew exactly what was going on and was just playing a part for Shinji's and Rei's benefit. I suspect the latter is more likely, even though the presentation of the scene makes it hard to swallow. But that aside, what Ornette said. Nobody knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 16, 2012 7:09 am Monk Ed wrote: Whenever the "casablanca theory" is presented, I always have to present my counter-take that I outlined in the 24` fanwank thread: Rewarch the scene, but this time, pretend you don't already know that the white giant is really Lilith. Also consider the phrase "body of Christ" -- sometimes body can mean just a part, not the whole. From there it all clicked rather easily into place for me. In my mind at least the plothole is much ado about nothing anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 16, 2012 1:16 pm Bagheera wrote: Yes, except--as I noted the last time you presented the idea--it doesn't work because MPEs. If a clone of Adam's body

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 16, 2012 3:46 pm Monk Ed wrote: Who's talking about clones? It's simple: He thinks the giant is the original husk regrown to full size (as does the audience barring foreknowledge) and that what's in Gendo is a tiny piece of it. No clones necessary. Perhaps this looks slightly different from what I've said before, but before, I presented a number of variations, whereas now, I'm sticking to one because a book once told me it's more persuasive that way.
